recon.score_axes_summary:
LIVE base score = scoring.score_candidate total + bonus_* assembled at dispatch_pipeline.py:4228 + final_score at :4271. wave_scoring.py is NOT live (ENABLE_WAVE_SCORING=False; its own docstring l.11 says "Live scoring.score_candidate NIE przekazuje wave_adjustment"), so its boosts are inert.

AXES THAT TRACK TRUE OBJECTIVE (R6≤35 / committed / new-late):
- bonus_r6_soft_pen (dispatch_pipeline.py:3417 via _r6_soft_penalty l.626; const BAG_TIME_SOFT_MIN=30, PER_MIN=8, danger >32 extra -16/min, common.py:628-641) — penalizes worst-bag-delivery R6. This IS the objective; it is the DOMINANT axis (e.g. -203 on best 179 / -1350 on saturated 529).
- The committed-time + new-late objective is NOT in the additive score at all; it is enforced by the LATE-PICKUP TIER SORT (l.4991-5036, _late_pickup_tier l.495: committed_breach→tier2, needs_extension→tier1) which gates AFTER score. That is the correct primary protection.

AXES THAT PUSH SCORE IN OTHER (non-objective) DIRECTIONS — the miscalibration sources:
- bonus_r8_soft_pen (pickup span, l.3889) + bonus_r9_stopover (-8/stop) scale with BAG SIZE, so a courier with a bigger PRODUCTIVE bag (better chaining, lower R6) is penalized more. 480857: dom 123 bag=4 R6=10.6 gets r8=-140.55/r9=-32 vs best 400 bag=3 R6=24.2 r8=-131.55/r9=-24 → 123 scores -326 < 400 -273 despite winning the objective on every axis.
- bonus_v3273_wait_courier (idle-pre-pickup penalty, in sum l.4228) — penalizes waiting at restaurant even when that courier is geometrically perfect (480857 dom -41.53; 481156 dom -53.95; 481340 best 179 -66.44).
- v326_fleet_load_adjustment (-15 on loaded 123, 480857) penalizes being loaded — opposite of fleet-utilization objective.
- v325_new_courier_penalty (l.1482) = flat -50 (LOW_ADVANTAGE, common.py:1754) applied to any speed-tier-'new' courier even AFTER the 30-delivery ramp (481156 dom 531 has 171 deliveries, ramp.active=False, yet -50). advantage=cand.score-max_non_new (l.1475) is only a bracket selector, NOT a score term.
- bonus_coordinator_idle (l.3861-3865) = -100 when is_coordinator & not coordinator_active.
Net: a pre_shift FICTION (179 in 481156, BIALYSTOK_CENTER, R6=37.4 breach, new_late=27.79) keeps score -176 (empty bag → r8/r9=0, the V3.16 "empty baseline ~82" asymmetry) and BEATS a real on-time courier (531 R6=15.7 late=1.0 score -315) purely on load/new-courier non-objective penalties.
recon.override_mechanisms:
best != raw-score-top via three post-scoring re-sort passes (all AFTER the score sort at dispatch_pipeline.py:4961):
1. _demote_blind_empty (l.1864, called l.4989) — POSITION-BUCKET override: if top-1 is blind+empty (no_gps/pre_shift, bag0) and an informed-pos alt exists, reorder informed>other>blind+empty (l.1884), ignoring score. This is the "best_effort/pos-bucket" resort.
2. LATE-PICKUP TIER GATING (l.4991-5036, gated ENABLE_LATE_PICKUP_HARD_GATE=ON, common.py:2409). Sort key (l.5029 via _late_pickup_score_first_key, or l.5034 fallback) puts committed-breach tier2 LAST regardless of score. This is the intended committed-time protection and is the mechanism that SHOULD have prevented the E2 cases.
3. E2 _pln_pure_resort (l.863, called l.5206 when ENABLE_E2_PLN_AB ON for order_id%5==0). It re-sorts top by pure pln_v (l.919-920 legacy key = (_pln_ord, orig)), CLOBBERING the tier order from #2. This is the bug: it runs LAST and undoes the tier gate.
Tier gating (l.5002) operates on `feasible` BEFORE top is sliced (l.5138); E2 (l.5206) operates on `top` AFTER, so it wins. Confirmed flip via metric pln_ab_flipped=True on both 481340 (best 179) and 481080 (best 413).
Also: KOORD gate (l.5347-5383) evaluates _gate_score_excluding_ranking_deltas (l.5361) < MIN_PROPOSE_SCORE=-100 (common.py:651) → demotes whole decision to KOORD unless _always_propose_on (l.1975, flag ENABLE_ALWAYS_PROPOSE_ON_SATURATION=True live).
recon.coordinator_policy:
bonus_coordinator_idle = -100.0 at dispatch_pipeline.py:3861-3865, fires only when is_coordinator=True AND coordinator_active=False (added to bonus_penalty_sum l.4228). NOT too aggressive vs committed-breach, proven on the window:
(a) Arithmetic: a clean idle coordinator's baseline ~+100 (123 in 481340 scored +99.86 with idle_pen=0) minus 100 = ~-0.14, still FAR above any committed-breaker because the R6 soft penalty already sinks breakers very low (179 = -410.7). So -100 cannot push a clean coordinator below a ~20min-committed-breaking alternative.
(b) Empirical: across all 165 dominated_cases, ZERO cases where an idle coordinator was demoted -100 WHILE the chosen best broke committed (measured: scanned full records for is_coordinator & bonus_coordinator_idle<=-50 & best.late_pickup_committed_breach=True → 0 hits).
(c) Of the 33 cases where cid=123 dominates, when 123 actually surfaces as a feasible alternative it is is_coordinator=False (7 cases, e.g. 481340 — not a coordinator at all there) or coordinator_active=True with idle_pen=0 (3 cases, e.g. 480857 — lost on r8/r9/v3273/fleet_load, NOT on -100); 23 have no full record at the case ts. So the -100 demote is NOT the mechanism behind the inspectable 123-dominances. CONCLUSION: -100 idle policy is sound for the window; flag policy_review only on the by-design reservation cases, not as a committed-breach driver.
recon.e2_fix_explanation:
Both E2-sig cases are the E2-pln pure-resort bug and Fix B fully covers them; Fix C adds margin. Window (06-10..16) predates the fix commits 2454f26 (B) + bdb9b79 (C) landed 06-17 ~01:35; both flags now ON in flags.json (ENABLE_PLN_RESORT_WITHIN_TIER=True, ENABLE_PLN_QUALITY_AWARE=True).
481340 ts=2026-06-16T17:46: best=179 (pln_ab_arm=pln, pln_ab_flipped=True, late_pickup_committed_breach=True→tier2, committed_max=19.89, R6=39.8) was selected over dom=123 (committed_breach=False→tier0, R6=12.7, new_late=1.0, score +99.86, is_coordinator=False, bonus_coordinator_idle=0.0). The pln_ab_flipped=True proves _pln_pure_resort moved a tier2 breaker to top[0].
481080 ts=2026-06-15T15:24: best=413 (pln_ab_flipped=True, committed_breach=True→tier2, committed_max=13.64, R6=47.1) over dom=484 (committed_breach=False→tier0, R6=40.1, score -455 > -665, idle=0.0).
Fix B sort key (dispatch_pipeline.py:917): (1 if _late_pickup_tier(c)==2 else 0, _bucket(c), pln_key, orig). Since dom is tier0 and best is tier2, the (0,...) tuple sorts the dominator strictly before the tier2 best → top[0] flips BACK to the non-breaking dominator. Holds for both. Fix C (_pln_ord_quality l.897-905) further subtracts PLN_QUALITY_R6_COEFF*r6_breach + late penalty within-tier, so 179/413's R6 breach (39.8 / 47.1) is also penalized in pln — defense in depth. Neither dominator is a coordinator-reservation case (idle=0.0), so classification is E2_fixed, not coordinator_reservation. No regression risk: fix only constrains the resort to within-tier.
noncoord_rootcause_hypotheses: BAG-SIZE LOAD OVER-WEIGHTING (primary, strongest evidence). bonus_r8_soft_pen (l.3889) + bonus_r9_stopover (-8/stop) scale with bag size, so a courier ALREADY carrying a productive bag that chains the new order cheaply (low R6) is penalized for the bag itself. 480857: dom 123 bag=4 R6=10.6 new_late=1.0 dominates best 400 bag=3 R6=24.2 new_late=11.5 on EVERY objective axis but scores -326 < -273 because r8(-140.55 vs -131.55)+r9(-32 vs -24)+v3273_wait(-41.53 vs 0)+fleet_load(-15 vs 0) ≈ -73 of non-objective load penalty. The score axis miscalibrated = R8/R9/fleet-load magnitude relative to R6.

STALE SPEED-TIER 'new' LABEL → flat -50 long after graduation. v325_new_courier_penalty (l.1482, V325_NEW_COURIER_PENALTY_LOW_ADVANTAGE=-50 common.py:1754) is applied to any candidate tagged cs_tier_label=='new' even when new_courier_ramp.active=False (deliveries>=30). 481156: dom 531 has 171 deliveries (ramp off) yet carries -50 + v3273_wait -53.95 = -104 non-objective, dropping it (R6 15.7, on-time) below a pre_shift fiction (179, R6 37.4 breach, +27.79 late, score -176). Axis miscalibrated = v325 new-courier penalty applied past graduation (tier label decoupled from delivery count).

PRE_SHIFT / EMPTY-BAG BASELINE ASYMMETRY (the original V3.16 lesson, still live as score). An empty-bag pre_shift candidate gets r8=r9=v3273=0 so it floats near the +82 baseline even at BIALYSTOK_CENTER fiction with an R6 breach, while real loaded couriers eat load penalties. _demote_blind_empty (l.1864) only fires when the blind+empty cand is top-1 AND an informed alt is top-eligible; when score already ranks a loaded real courier below the fiction the demote does not rescue it. Axis: synthetic-position candidates are under-penalized on the objective (R6 breach not strong enough to offset their zero-load advantage). Mechanism is structural, not a coordinator demote.

WAIT_COURIER (bonus_v3273_wait_courier) double-counts. It penalizes idle-before-pickup, but the true objective already prices lateness via R6/new-late; a courier optimally positioned to wait a few minutes for a fresh pickup (lowest R6) is the RIGHT pick yet gets a large negative (480857 -41.53, 481156 -53.95, 481340 best -66.44). Hypothesis: v3273 magnitude over-weights idle minutes relative to delivery freshness.
recon.ramp_policy: new_courier_ramp / v325 penalty in _v325_new_courier_penalty (dispatch_pipeline.py:1348). Two layers: (1) SP-B2-RAMPA (l.1453-1469 area, ENABLE_NEW_COURIER_RAMP=True, NEW_COURIER_RAMP_DELIVERIES=30): for couriers with <30 deliveries, only "ramp" runs allowed (km_to_pickup<=2.5, bag==0, slot not 14-17); off-profile runs get score+=NEW_COURIER_RAMP_MALUS=-20 (l.43 of fn) or hard-skip when bag>=V325_NEW_COURIER_BAG_HARD_SKIP_AT (l.1456, score=NEG_INF). (2) Post-ramp graduated discount (l.1470-1492): advantage=cand.score-max_non_new (l.1475) selects bracket → penalty HIGH=-10 (adv>=50) / MED=-30 (20-50) / LOW=-50 (<20) at common.py:1752-1754, applied to score l.1482. SOLO-GUARD (l.1500-1521, NEW_COURIER_RAMP_SOLO_MALUS=-60): if all feasible <MIN_PROPOSE and only a ramp-blocked courier exists, restore pre_block + -60 so it stays proposable (human-gated) instead of silent KOORD. MISCALIBRATION: the LOW=-50 penalty hits speed-tier-'new'-labeled couriers regardless of having graduated the 30-delivery ramp (481156 dom 531 = 171 deliveries, ramp.active=False, still -50).
recon.notes:
READ-ONLY; no code/flags/services changed. PY=/root/.openclaw/venvs/dispatch/bin/python; casetool + raw shadow_decisions.jsonl used.

Key files: /root/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/dispatch_v2/dispatch_pipeline.py (selection+scoring), common.py (flags/consts). wave_scoring.py is INERT (ENABLE_WAVE_SCORING=False) — true live score axes are bonus_* assembled at dispatch_pipeline.py:4228 + scoring.score_candidate.

CLASSIFICATION of the 3 probed buckets:
- 481340, 481080 = E2_fixed (pln_ab_flipped=True, dominator idle=0.0, NOT coordinator). Fix B flips both back; Fix C adds R6-breach penalty in pln. Confirmed pre-fix window.
- 480857 = score_miscalibration_REAL. Winning axis lost: bag-size load penalties (r8/r9) + v3273_wait + v326_fleet_load over-weight vs R6. Dom 123 here is coordinator_active=True (idle_pen=0) so -100 is NOT involved.
- 481156 = score_miscalibration_REAL (+ borderline by-design). Dom 531 sunk by stale v325 'new' -50 (171 deliveries, ramp off) + v3273_wait -53.95; best 179 is a pre_shift FICTION with R6 breach kept afloat by empty-bag zero-load. The pre_shift fiction beating a real on-time courier is the V3.16 baseline asymmetry resurfacing.
- 481196 = saturation_leastbad (pool_feas=0, all feasibility=NO). best 529 R6=87.6 is genuinely worse than dom 413 (R6 48.9) / 531 (R6 73.3) BUT those have fifo_violations / sla_violation hidden disqualifiers; bonus_r9_wait_pen=-2166 dominates 529's score. Outcome delivered by cid=123 (operator took over). Marginal — not a clean bug; the score magnitudes here are saturation artifacts, classify saturation_leastbad with note that 413's lower R6 suggests least-bad pick could be 413 not 529 (worth a single-case look, not systemic).

COORDINATOR -100: not too aggressive — 0/165 cases where an idle-demoted coordinator coexisted with a committed-breaking best; arithmetic shows -100 lands a clean coordinator at ~-0.14, still above R6-sunk breakers (~-410). The 33 "123 dominator" cases are NOT driven by -100 (123 is non-coordinator or active in every inspectable one).

BIGGEST REAL MISCALIBRATION (recommend Adrian decision, not in scope to fix): bag-size-coupled load penalties (r8/r9/fleet_load) + stale v325 'new' label + empty-bag/pre_shift zero-load baseline asymmetry, which together let a fictional pre_shift or a lighter bag outscore a real, on-time, low-R6, productively-loaded courier. 111/118 dominators having LOWER score is consistent with this: the objective (R6/committed) is enforced by the tier GATE, not by score, so anything the gate doesn't catch (tier ties, non-breach) is decided by load/new-courier axes that point away from the objective.
